subject: Add acumos adapter project
commit_message:
Add acumos adapter project

Signed-off-by: Andrew Gauld <agauld@att.com>
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1860
Change-Id: Ib22fd2aa61fe7761bacf85e69540d11803c7acee
Signed-off-by: Andrew Gauld <agauld@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Is this making a new subfolder called "adapter"?

Patch Set 1:

if we want to be correct, we should keep the name "adapter"

if we want to be consistent, on the other hand, it should be named "adaptor" given two other ONAP projects use this:

ccsdk/sli/adaptors
sdnc/adaptors

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

We have been writing this code internally, thus I have no further comments since this has already been extensively reviewed by our team.

my only two comments here relate 1) to what repo this is in in ONAP and 2) on a CICD dependency that may not work at first.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

I'll go with correct over duplicating a misspelling in some other component.  Vijay has said this should go in dcaegen/platform, yes this creates a directory "adapter" to hold adapters for importing microservices from other systems and creates a directory for the first type of adapter, the Acumos adapter.

Patch Set 1:

(5 comments)

No docs/ area?

Patch Set 1:

"You're not in Kansas anymore"

AFAIK, ONAP DCAE does not, in general, do the docs thing. We don't even use .rst for the most part. I've tried explaining before that ONAP DCAE is on some.. "old processes".


examples of existing platform repos that have all been in existence for some time, and are all heavily used, without a docs dir:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=dcaegen2/platform/plugins.git;a=tree;f=k8s;h=07281211e81c2f576438a95f46a74581340508b0;hb=6b7857037b8c82471395a929aba950677693d151

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=dcaegen2/platform/configbinding.git;a=tree;h=ad4fcc93b03491efda01e76925b4091abdab6d97;hb=ad4fcc93b03491efda01e76925b4091abdab6d97

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=dcaegen2/utils.git;a=tree;f=dcaeapplib;h=fecb12ec0ab4b801f2a6800845ec9c4ab1eb920b;hb=8060697f3634bd21a66347114160ed416a9f617e

One notable exception seems to be the CLI tool which does..

Patch Set 1:

As Jack noted, all DCAE component release documentation are maintained under dcaegen2 repo. For all new MOD components - this should be done also by M3/M4 (DCAEGEN2-1865)
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

I've fixed the 2 botched lines.

I'm trying to parse the rest of your comments.

I think what you're saying is that:

1) The live documentation is actually in a separate repository (dcaegen2) and that, therefore, it can't be delivered in this code (so no need to worry about it beyond putting enough in here to keep the developer's sanity) and,

2) Python going into a Docker image doesn't need a long description.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> I've fixed the 2 botched lines.
> 
> I'm trying to parse the rest of your comments.
> 
> I think what you're saying is that:
> 
> 1) The live documentation is actually in a separate repository (dcaegen2) and that, therefore, it can't be delivered in this code (so no need to worry about it beyond putting enough in here to keep the developer's sanity) and,
> 
> 2) Python going into a Docker image doesn't need a long description.

I agree here, but Chris is the one who raised the original concerns.

Patch Set 2:

I want to note that, due to the timestamp issue that we raised to Vijay, this POM is currently insufficient for CICD. The global docker jjb is not currently used in ONAP DCAE. I believe we are waiting for Vijay to clarify whether we need the timestamps or not. 
If we do, this pom will have to grow (by a lot).

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> I want to note that, due to the timestamp issue that we raised to Vijay, this POM is currently insufficient for CICD. The global docker jjb is not currently used in ONAP DCAE. I believe we are waiting for Vijay to clarify whether we need the timestamps or not. 
> If we do, this pom will have to grow (by a lot).

and well need the maven-phase-script as well

my strong hope is that we do NOT have to go down this route, and that we can use the global jjb for Docker.

Patch Set 2:

Yes, all containers in ONAP must include timestamp based tagging (in addition to latest/SNAPSHOT).

Patch Set 2:

Vijay,

Very well.

I have absolutely no clue how to meet your requirement.  If you provide the exact verbatum text to do so, I will add it to whatever places you tell me it must go.

Since your requirement also prevents using the standard LF global CI-management rules for building Docker images, I also expect you to make the appropriate ci-management changes.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I thought I saw you guys discussing this already, this seems like the wrong destination for your seed code.  All the other DCAEGEN2 platform items seem to be in subrepositories.  For example I've helped Tommy with gerrit.onap.org:29418/dcaegen2/platform/configbinding.git.  Just possibly this one should go into something like gerrit.onap.org:29418/dcaegen2/platform/aoadapter.git  ??

So because the dcaegen2-platform repository has no code right now, it only has build tools like the much beloved mvn-phase-script.sh :) I'm voting -1 on adding code to it.

Patch Set 2:

Vijay please respond to Chris' "-1" since you were the one who insisted that this had to go here and not in a sub-repository.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

The repo/subproject structure was documented in the jira - not sure what the issue is?  Although original proposal was acumos/adapter which Andrew modified to adapter/acumos; I'm okay with this change and structure.

Patch Set 3:

The merge failed because the push it being done to 10004 (docker.staging) which ONAP does not really use. ONAP has only used 10003 to push snapshots and staging images

Patch Set 3:

submitted https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ci-management/+/98537 to fix this push registry
